untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options market data (open buy volume on options contracts, minimum_open_buy_contracts threshold) which is not available in the allowed indicator list. The indicator set only contains equity price/volume indicators and derivatives thereof (moving averages, RSI, ATR, MACD, etc.) but lacks options-specific metrics such as open interest, put/call ratios, options volume, or implied volatility. Additionally, portfolio formation based on cross-market information transmission from options to underlying stocks cannot be accurately modeled with available indicators.
translation_notes: The core signal requires: (1) options open buy volume data—not available, (2) options contract counts—not available, (3) size-group classification (market cap segmentation)—not available, (4) cross-market regime detection (options→equity transmission)—not available. A proxy using equity volume alone would fundamentally misrepresent the signal. The 5-day holding period and day-1 effect emphasis are translatable, but without the foundational options data, the rule cannot be meaningfully implemented.
